summary: @across-toolkit/eslint-config postinstall exfiltrates cloud credentials and SSH keys to webhook.site
details: On npm install, postinstall.js reads AWS and GCP instance metadata (http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/ and http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token), harvests ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.aws/config, gcloud application default credentials, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.npmrc, ~/.gitconfig, /etc/environment, /proc/1/environ, and.env files, and collects os.hostname(), whoami output, and the full process.env including NPM_TOKEN. The collected data is base64-encoded and POSTed via https.request to a hardcoded webhook.site URL (https://webhook.site/a585f4ec-20f7-4bd1-bac7-f3e53799dc5f). Package name and version (@across-toolkit/eslint-config@99.0.0, author 'across-protocol') impersonate the Across Protocol brand.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
